articles.title: How China’s Weapons Transfers to Iran Have Evolved Over Decades
articles.summary: China has denied claims this month by U.S. intelligence that it might have shipped arms to Iran. In recent years, China has supported Iran with dual-use parts.

articles.title: Trump’s Blockade Risks Upending an Emerging Détente With China
articles.summary: In a thinly veiled critique of the war in Iran, China’s leader said the world could not risk reverting “to the law of the jungle.”

articles.title: Pope heads to Cameroon as separatists announce 3-day pause in fighting
articles.summary: Pope Leo XIV is heading to the central African nation of Cameroon with a message of peace for its separatist region and for talks with President Paul Biya.

articles.title: They counted on a rural dialysis unit to keep them alive. Then it closed
articles.summary: A hospital in Nebraska shut down the only dialysis unit for miles, upending lives. That's despite a new federal program that gave the state more than $200 million to improve rural health care access.

articles.title: No one likes being discombobulated. How did the feeling get such a fun name?
articles.summary: Sometimes you just need to recombobulate. That word isn't in the dictionary, but it is on a beloved sign at Milwaukee's airport.

articles.title: Quit a GLP-1? Plan to start again? It's not recommended, but plenty of people do it
articles.summary: A majority of people who start the obesity and diabetes medicines known as GLP-1s also quit them, and plan to restart again. Research hasn't yet shown the health impacts of cycling on and off the drug

articles.title: 'No peace': Nearly a year after her son's death, she learned that ICE was responsible
articles.summary: Ruben Ray Martinez is considered the first person to be killed by ICE during President Trump's second term. His mother believes his death could have been avoided.

articles.title: Vance Says Pope Leo Should Be More Careful When Talking About Theology
articles.summary: The vice president, who is Catholic, took issue with Pope Leo XIV’s statement that disciples of Christ are “never on the side of those who once wielded the sword and today drop bombs.”

articles.title: Morning news brief
articles.summary: Inside the peace talks happening across the Middle East, Iran war drives Europe toward fuel crisis, Eric Swalwell facing new sexual assault allegations.

articles.title: As fuel prices continue to rise, Iran war drives Europe toward new crisis
articles.summary: As fuel protests bring Ireland to a standstill and UK leaders decry price hikes driven by the Iran war, Europe -- barely recovered from the energy shock of the Ukraine war -- faces another crisis.

articles.title: IMF predicts Iran war will slow economic growth and raise inflation globally
articles.summary: The International Monetary Fund expects the Iran war to slow the global economy and raise inflation. NPR's Michel Martin discusses the forecast with Simon Johnson, a former IMF chief economist.

articles.title: Trump slams Italian prime minister, one of his few remaining European allies
articles.summary: NPR's A Martinez speaks with Italian journalist Viviana Mazza about President Trump's criticism of Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni, one of few remaining allies in Europe.

articles.title: Raffle winner thrilled to claim a $1 million Picasso with a $117 ticket
articles.summary: "How do I check that it's not a hoax?" said Ari Hodara. The Parisian art enthusiast could not believe his luck when he found out he'd won a Pablo Picasso painting worth $1 million.

articles.title: Super Typhoon Sinlaku pounds remote U.S. islands in the Pacific Ocean
articles.summary: A super typhoon steadily battered a pair of remote U.S. islands in the Pacific Ocean with ferocious winds and relentless rains, shredding tin roofs and forcing residents to take cover.

articles.title: Pope Leo’s Challenge in Cameroon: Show African Catholics How Much They Matter
articles.summary: Catholicism is growing fast on the continent, yet Africans play a comparatively small role in church leadership. Cameroon, which Leo XIV will visit Wednesday, shows the divide.

articles.title: Swalwell’s Exit Injects ‘Chaos’ Into California Governor’s Race
articles.summary: Democratic candidates see sudden voter interest in the sleepy contest as a campaign opportunity. All are scrambling for support from former backers of Eric Swalwell.

articles.title: Gallego Dismissed Rumors of a ‘Flirty’ Swalwell, Highlighting a Culture of Silence
articles.summary: The admission by Senator Ruben Gallego that he had heard, but disbelieved, rumors about Eric Swalwell and women showed the attitude on Capitol Hill toward men accused of behaving badly.

articles.title: Rubio Hosts Israel and Lebanon for Rare Meeting Shadowed by U.S.-Iran War
articles.summary: The gathering ended with encouraging words, even as Israel continued to refuse to halt its military campaign against Iran-backed Hezbollah in Lebanon.

articles.title: A New Accuser Says Eric Swalwell Sexually Assaulted Her
articles.summary: The woman said Mr. Swalwell, who resigned from Congress on Tuesday afternoon, raped her in a West Hollywood hotel room in 2018. She said she believed she was drugged.

articles.title: Israel is building a buffer zone inside Lebanon
articles.summary: Israel is creating a large buffer zone in southern Lebanon for a prolonged military occupation, with low expectations that direct talks with Lebanon will lead to quick action on disarming Hezbollah.

articles.title: Veteran diplomat offers insights into the war in Iran — and thoughts on what's next
articles.summary: The war entered a new phase when President Trump began a U.S. naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz. Aaron David Miller of the Carnegie Endowment for International Peace explains what this means.

articles.title: After losing loved ones, an Israeli and a Palestinian work together for Middle East peace
articles.summary: An Israeli whose parents were killed on Oct. 7, 2023, and a Palestinian whose brother died from injuries in Israeli custody say they've become like brothers. Their new book is The Future Is Peace: A S

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
